<insights>

- High cohesion (0.96) — files are tightly interconnected
- 14 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- At 349 files with cohesion 0.96, this is a densely connected but very large zone — the high cohesion reflects strong internal coupling rather than clean modular boundaries, and sub-zone extraction along the server/viewer boundary would improve navigability
- Contains both server-side code (routes, gateways, services) and client-side viewer code (components, hooks, views), which reflects the web package's dual nature as a server that also builds a static client
- The rex-gateway.ts and domain-gateway.ts chokepoints are correctly located at this zone's boundary, making cross-package imports explicit and auditable
- At 349 files this is the largest zone in the codebase by a wide margin; while high cohesion (0.96) reflects genuine connectivity, the zone spans the full server/viewer split and would benefit from architectural sub-zone extraction to improve targeted refactoring.
- The rex-gateway.ts and domain-gateway.ts files are co-located within the core zone alongside viewer components — these are architecturally critical chokepoints and are well-placed at the zone's composition root.
- 6 imports flow from this zone back into the viewer-messaging-pipeline zone, forming a bidirectional relationship; these should be audited to confirm they pass through external.ts rather than bypassing the gateway contract.
- Zone "web-application-core" has files across 20 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- server/ files have zero runtime imports from src/viewer/ — the 3 web-server→web-viewer cross-zone edges noted earlier are confirmed as non-existent at the file level, indicating the zone graph statistic was a measurement artifact
- The web package is responsible for all but 2 of the 15 cross-zone edges in the entire codebase — rex contributes only the fix↔rex-unit pair, confirming web is the sole architectural complexity hotspot in the monorepo
- web-viewer's 37 outbound imports to web-server is the single largest cross-zone edge in the entire codebase by a factor of 5x over the next largest (web→web-viewer at 16) — this asymmetric consumer relationship is intentional but warrants monitoring as viewer feature count grows
- The previously flagged web-server→web-viewer 3 cross-zone imports are confirmed absent at the file level — no server/ file imports from src/viewer/ at runtime. The zone algorithm likely attributed shared-directory proximity or build config references as import edges. This finding can be downgraded from warning to resolved.
- web-viewer→web-server with 37 cross-zone imports is 5x larger than the next largest edge in the codebase — the viewer is a heavy consumer of server-side infrastructure. While directionally correct (viewer consumes, server does not import viewer), the volume suggests server utilities may be leaking implementation details rather than exporting a clean viewer-facing API surface.
- The web package accounts for 13 of the 15 cross-zone edges in the entire codebase. Every other package (rex, hench, sourcevision, llm-client) contributes at most one bidirectional pair, indicating web's internal zone topology is categorically more complex than all other packages combined.
- The zone contains src/cli/index.ts — a spawnable CLI entrypoint — alongside HTTP server routes and Preact viewer components, creating three distinct execution contexts (CLI subprocess, HTTP server process, browser bundle) within a single zone. Changes to the CLI entrypoint carry an inflated apparent blast radius because the zone metric includes 348 unrelated server and viewer files.
- web → web-server: 2 imports alongside web → web-viewer: 16 imports shows the composition layer distributes its setup concerns across two different target zones rather than a single composition root, suggesting the 'web' zone conflates build-config files with runtime initialization files without a declared boundary between them.
- src/cli/index.ts is co-located in the same zone as HTTP server routes and Preact viewer components despite representing a distinct execution context (spawnable CLI binary). This conflation makes it impossible to assess per-execution-context blast radius and obscures whether the CLI entrypoint has leaked runtime imports from server or viewer code.
- 'web-application-core' is a residual accumulator zone name — unlike 'task-usage-scheduler', 'viewer-messaging-pipeline', and 'web-landing-page' which each name a specific bounded context, 'core' signals that the zone contains everything not classified elsewhere. Residual zone names reliably predict zones that grow unboundedly because their implicit boundary rule is 'all remaining files' rather than a domain-specific criterion.
- The zone's 14 [entrypoint]-archetyped files span all three execution contexts (CLI subprocess, HTTP server, browser bundle) with no documented relationship between entry points. Entry-point count is a proxy for public API surface width — 14 entry points in a single zone with no sub-zone decomposition means external consumers can couple into this zone from 14 different directions simultaneously.
- Zone name 'web-application-core' is a residual accumulator with no bounded-context semantics. As sub-zones are extracted (e.g. 'web-server-routes', 'web-viewer-components'), the remaining 'core' zone should be renamed to reflect only what it actually contains at that point rather than serving as a permanent catch-all. Without a rename, the zone will continue to absorb newly added files by default.
- 14 [entrypoint] files within a single zone with no documented API surface contract between them creates an unbounded coupling surface. Establish a zone health rule: zones with more than 3 entry points should either be decomposed or have their entry points explicitly grouped into named API surfaces. The current 14-entry-point count is the highest in the codebase by a factor of 4x over the next largest zone.
- The 9 reverse call-graph edges from task-usage-scheduler → web-application-core represent genuine runtime callbacks, not zone-boundary artifacts. These 9 calls should be extracted to an event/callback interface that task-usage-scheduler can implement without importing from web-application-core directly, converting the bidirectional runtime dependency to a unidirectional observer pattern.
- The 67 call-graph calls from viewer-messaging-pipeline into web-application-core indicate messaging infrastructure has absorbed application-layer logic. Audit the 67 call sites to identify which are data transformations (belong in messaging) vs domain operations (belong in web-application-core), then extract domain operations back to the application layer with messaging calling through a defined callback interface.
- Zone "web-viewer" has files across 20 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- File "packages/web/src/viewer/external.ts" is pinned to zone "Web Viewer" but lives in packages/web/src/viewer/ — consider moving to packages/web/src/viewer/styles/ to align physical location with architectural zone
- The move-file finding for packages/web/src/viewer/external.ts (pinned to web-viewer, flagged for relocation to styles/) is incorrect: external.ts is the canonical viewer-side gateway documented in CLAUDE.md and must remain at packages/web/src/viewer/external.ts. The automated heuristic misidentified this as a misplaced file; no relocation should occur.
- Zone finding #4 (move-file: external.ts → styles/) is definitively invalidated by CLAUDE.md's Gateway modules table, which explicitly documents external.ts as the canonical viewer-side gateway that must remain at packages/web/src/viewer/external.ts. Finding #2 correctly identifies this. The warning severity on finding #4 is unjustified — acting on it would break the documented gateway contract. Finding #4 should be treated as suppressed.
- The 14-entry-point wide API surface (finding #0) is expected for the hub zone: web-viewer is the composition center receiving 11+ distinct cross-zone edges per CLAUDE.md. The heuristic threshold was calibrated for spoke zones, not hubs.
- Finding #4 (warning: move external.ts to styles/) is directly contradicted by finding #2 and by CLAUDE.md documentation. The warning severity is unjustified. No relocation should occur. Treat finding #4 as suppressed with info severity.
